feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Women hit fertility tipping point at 49 even with young donor eggs
entry.summary: Age-related changes in the womb lining may be the cause and could be treatable in the future, say experts.

entry.title: 2 Teas With Surprising Gut Health Benefits, According to a New Study
entry.summary: A new study finds green and oolong teas offer standout gut health and antioxidant benefits when used to brew kombucha. Learn what to sip for more benefits.

entry.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak in Michigan grows to nearly 700 cases
entry.summary: Haley Hompstead, a Monroe County resident — an area identified as a hotspot for the infection — said her symptoms began July 3.

entry.title: What to know as ticks spread to new regions and bring new threats
entry.summary: It’s summertime, and ticks are out in full force. While in certain parts of the country, ticks have long been an issue, they’re now spreading to new regions and bringing new threats. William Brangham discussed the blood-sucking terrors with Holly Gaff, a mat…

entry.title: ‘Don’t Die’ Guy Bryan Johnson Says He Has Serious Autoimmune Disease
entry.summary: "My stomach is eating itself," wrote Johnson.

entry.title: California Man’s Eyelid Bump Turned Out To Be a Worm Never Before Seen in the US
entry.summary: The eight millimeter-long "nodule" on the man's eyelid was originally suspected to be a chalazion.

entry.title: NYC's Upper East Side experiencing cluster of Legionnaires' with at least 18 cases
entry.summary: The health commissioner says there are several critical patients in the Upper East Side outbreak.

entry.title: County Health Officials Report First Locally Acquired Chagas Case
entry.summary: County health officials have confirmed the region’s first locally acquired case of Chagas disease, identified during routine blood donation screening in a donor without symptoms.

entry.title: 10 ‘healthiest’ ultra-processed foods — the scientist’s list
entry.summary: Not all UPFs are bad for us. Here are the UPFs you can put in your trolley without a second thought

entry.title: Mom, 34, Gets Pregnant with ‘Miracle’ Baby After Breast Cancer. Then Doctors Discover 7 Brain Tumors
entry.summary: “I just don’t want to forget my life and my kids, I just don’t want to lose that,” Amy Carroll said ahead of her treatment for brain cancer

entry.title: Explosive diarrhea? Here's what to know about Cyclospora
entry.summary: It's invisible to the naked eye, difficult to trace and can cause weeks of diarrhea. Here's what to know about the parasite, Cyclospora.

entry.title: Biohacker Bryan Johnson Spent Millions Trying to Live Forever. His Stomach Was Still Eating Itself.
entry.summary: Bryan Johnson's autoimmune gastritis diagnosis reveals how even a $2M annual health protocol can miss a silent, slow-moving disease for over a decade.

entry.title: Why you should rethink wearing contact lenses on airplanes
entry.summary: Doctors warn that wearing contacts on long-haul flights can lead to some serious issues.

entry.title: I built a house for my dad with dementia to live in with us. When his health declined, it cost $120,000 to support him.
entry.summary: John Nuar, 34, cared for his father with dementia before moving him into memory care. Nothing prepared him for the financial decisions he had to make.

entry.title: Scientists discover why exercise reverses muscle aging
entry.summary: Researchers have uncovered a molecular “switch” that helps explain why exercise keeps ageing muscles healthy. By reducing levels of a gene called DEAF1, physical activity allows older muscles to clear out damage, repair themselves, and maintain strength.

entry.title: Legionnaire’s Outbreak Hits Upper East Side—Central Park Visitors Should Monitor For Symptoms
entry.summary: Anyone who visited the impacted area, including the east side of Central Park, should monitor for symptoms.

entry.title: 5 Fruits High in Potassium That Lower Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Learn how fruits like bananas, oranges, and kiwis can support healthy blood pressure thanks to their high potassium content and other heart-healthy nutrients.

entry.title: Why experts say peptides just might be the secret to reviving thinning hair
entry.summary: Research suggests these short chains of amino acids can boost scalp circulation, promote collagen production and may even stimulate new growth.

entry.title: Cat Fleas Linked to Fatal Human Disease in Texas
entry.summary: The potentially life-threatening disease was nearly eradicated decades ago, but now it is back and taking on Texas.

entry.title: How Much Sunlight Do You Need for Healthy Vitamin D Levels?
entry.summary: How much sunlight do you actually need for vitamin D? Discover what affects vitamin D absorption and how to balance sun safety with vitamin D needs.

entry.title: Ebola deaths in Congo top 500 as health workers threaten to strike
entry.summary: Authorities report that at least 500 people have died from over 1,500 confirmed Ebola cases in Congo

entry.title: 6 Cat Breeds Known for Minimal Shedding
entry.summary: Looking for a cat with less shedding? These six breeds are known for leaving less fur around the house than most.

entry.title: Blind People Offered Hope of Seeing Again After Stem Cell Breakthrough Remakes Retinal Blood Vessels
entry.summary: When injected into mice with retinal disease, the special retinal blood cells integrated into the damaged tissue to regenerate blood vessels.

entry.title: Oncologists Warn: Ditch Talc-Based Powders to Lower Cancer Risk
entry.summary: Plus, healthier alternatives to try.

entry.title: Golden Retriever Gets a 'Naughty' Report Card From Day Care — and the Photos Are Priceless
entry.summary: This canine cutie got called out.

entry.title: What Happens To Your Brain When You Brush Your Teeth With Your Opposite Hand?
entry.summary: Brush your teeth with your non-dominant hand to support neuroplasticity and new neural connections. Facts and research.

entry.title: Healthy eating habits may protect your brain as you age
entry.summary: Healthy eating may help protect the brain, even after early biological changes linked to Alzheimer’s disease have begun, a study suggests. Dr. Leana Wen explains.

entry.title: Thomas J. O’Connor Animal Control and Adoption Center closed to the public due to virus outbreaks
entry.summary: The Thomas J. O'Connor Animal Control and Adoption Center in Springfield is closed to the public until further notice, due to an outbreak of two viruses.

entry.title: Doctors say 8 forgotten habits could help fight stress, obesity and chronic disease
entry.summary: From walking everywhere to eating seasonal foods and sharing community meals, experts say these forgotten American health habits are worth reviving.

entry.title: Column | Why does cancer seem so common right now? An oncologist explains.
entry.summary: While more people are being diagnosed with cancer today compared with 50 years ago, the good news is that fewer people are dying from it.

entry.title: Why U.S. measles outbreaks have grown harder to extinguish
entry.summary: The nation is already nearing last year’s record case total, and experts say the virus is forcing doctors to relearn a disease many thought had been consigned to history.

entry.title: Scientists Wipe Out 99% of Cancer Cells in Lab Using Vibrating Molecules
entry.summary: Scientists may have found a way to destroy cancer cells without initially relying on chemotherapy, surgery, or radiation.

entry.title: Help! My Husband Is Ignoring His Doctor’s Warnings. It Could Mean Life or Death.
entry.summary: I'm terrified.

entry.title: Parent advice: One day, my wife lost all interest in one of our children. It's broken our family.
entry.summary: Our son is fully aware of what happened.

entry.title: Is testosterone replacement a fix for the blahs of aging?
entry.summary: American men may soon find it easier to get testosterone therapy under a new federal proposal that would loosen restrictions on prescriptions.

entry.title: Millions may be getting the wrong cholesterol test
entry.summary: A new study suggests that apoB, a blood test that measures harmful cholesterol particles, is better than standard LDL cholesterol testing for deciding who needs more intensive treatment. Researchers found it could prevent more heart attacks and strokes while …

entry.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
entry.summary: Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.

entry.title: Fast walkers in their 80s cut their risk of cognitive decline by half, study finds
entry.summary: A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.

entry.title: A New Study Says There's More to Eating Fruits and Vegetables Than Counting Servings
entry.summary: Eating five servings of fruits and vegetables a day is a great start, but a new study suggests it's not just the amount of produce you eat that matters. The types of fruits and vegetables you choose matter, too, because only some are rich in flavanols, antiox…

entry.title: Father and his daughter both saved by the same heart surgeon
entry.summary: One dad shares his story as Alder Hey celebrates more than 75 years of children's heart surgery.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak containment hampered by USAID closure, experts say
entry.summary: Foreign aid cuts have "demonstrably worsened" the Ebola crisis, a virologist told CNBC.

entry.title: NYC health officials warn Central Park visitors after Legionnaires’ outbreak grows on Upper East Side
entry.summary: The outbreak is centered around several Upper East Side ZIP codes, with health officials warning residents and recent visitors to seek care quickly if they develop flu-like symptoms.

entry.title: He Was Terrified by His Girlfriend's Severe Medical Symptoms. When He Begged Her to See a Doctor, She Accused Him of "Being Mean."
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Scientists may have finally found how Alzheimer's kills brain cells
entry.summary: Researchers have identified a previously overlooked mechanism of brain cell death that appears to play a major role in Alzheimer's disease and frontotemporal dementia. The finding could lead to new treatments aimed at slowing neuron loss by interrupting …

entry.title: People Are Sharing The Things They Think Will Be “Inevitable Outcomes” Of GLP-1s Over The Next 20 Years
entry.summary: "Obesity will become a disease of poverty."

entry.title: Doctors Said It Was a Spider Bite, Then She Started To Lose Her Vision
entry.summary: Emma Selvidge, 19, told Newsweek that the complications of this terrifying ordeal have been “some of the hardest to live with.“

entry.title: Scientists discover an infuriating thing ovaries may start doing after menopause: study
entry.summary: New research has found the reproductive system goes through a surprising change that’s detrimental to women’s health in the long run.

entry.title: Reported cases of cyclosporiasis rise to 572 in Michigan
entry.summary: More than 100 new cases of cyclosporiasis were reported Saturday, bringing the total to 572 cases in Michigan.

entry.title: RFK Jr. wants you to get more preventive care. Some experts say that's a bad idea.
entry.summary: But the health secretary has allies among some patient advocates and makers of tests that detect disease.

entry.title: Which US state is ‘America’s food poisoning capital’ — and how the other 49 rank for risk
entry.summary: With millions of international visitors to the US for the World Cup, on top of the usual domestic travelers, this summer is a busy time for travel and dining out.
